title: Canonical queue lags additive Frontier completion records

observations.claim: The canonical ACTIVE_QUEUE.json contains completed Frontier items R-001 through R-004 but does not contain R-033 or R-034.
R-033 is recorded as completed in an additive queue-update artifact with committed module, test, evidence, and verification references.
R-034 is recorded as completed in an additive queue-update artifact with committed detector, test, evidence, and verification references.
Recent repository commits corroborate the R-033 and R-034 implementation sequence.

resolution.summary: The canonical queue is an older consolidated snapshot, while additive queue-update artifacts preserve later completed work. The later records supersede the canonical queue only for the current Frontier-work inventory; they do not invalidate the canonical file as a historical snapshot.
do_not_infer: Do not infer that R-033 or R-034 are absent merely because they are missing from the canonical queue.
Do not infer that the canonical queue was corrupted or intentionally falsified.
Do not infer live browser or iPhone verification from deterministic synthetic tests.
resolution.recommended_reconciliation: Build a deterministic queue materializer that merges the canonical snapshot with additive queue-update artifacts by item identifier, preserves source timestamps and blob SHAs, and emits conflicts instead of silently overwriting records.

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch the three indexed files at the recorded blob SHAs and the listed commits. Falsify this record if ACTIVE_QUEUE.json already contains R-033 and R-034 at its recorded blob, or if either additive artifact does not record the stated completed work. Resolve the divergence only after a consolidated queue artifact explicitly incorporates or rejects the additive records with provenance.
